verses.latin_vulgate: Cum sanare vellem Israël,  revelata est iniquitas Ephraim,  et malitia Samariæ,  quia operati sunt mendacium ;  et fur ingressus est spolians,  latrunculus foris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּרׇפְאִ֣י לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל וְנִגְלָ֞ה עֲוֺ֤ן אֶפְרַ֙יִם֙ וְרָע֣וֹת שֹׁמְר֔וֹן כִּ֥י פָעֲל֖וּ שָׁ֑קֶר וְגַנָּ֣ב יָב֔וֹא פָּשַׁ֥ט גְּד֖וּד בַּחֽוּץ׃
verses.king_james: When I would have healed Israel, then the iniquity of Ephraim was discovered, and the wickedness of Samaria: for they commit falsehood; and the thief cometh in, and the troop of robbers spoileth without.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ne forte dicant in cordibus suis,  omnem malitiam eorum me recordatum,  nunc circumdederunt eos adinventiones suæ :  coram facie mea factæ sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבַל־יֹֽאמְרוּ֙ לִלְבָבָ֔ם כׇּל־רָעָתָ֖ם זָכָ֑רְתִּי עַתָּה֙ סְבָב֣וּם מַעַלְלֵיהֶ֔ם נֶ֥גֶד פָּנַ֖י הָיֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: And they consider not in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness: now their own doings have beset them about; they are before my face.

verses.latin_vulgate: In malitia sua lætificaverunt regem,  et in mendaciis suis principes.
verses.king_james: They make the king glad with their wickedness, and the princes with their lies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes adulterantes,  quasi clibanus succensus a coquente ;  quievit paululum civitas a commistione fermenti,  donec fermentaretur totum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֻּלָּם֙ מְנָ֣אֲפִ֔ים כְּמ֣וֹ תַנּ֔וּר בֹּעֵ֖רָה מֵאֹפֶ֑ה יִשְׁבּ֣וֹת מֵעִ֔יר מִלּ֥וּשׁ בָּצֵ֖ק עַד־חֻמְצָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: They are all adulterers, as an oven heated by the baker, who ceaseth from raising after he hath kneaded the dough, until it be leavened.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dies regis nostri : cœperunt principes furere a vino ;  extendit manum suam cum illusoribus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: י֣וֹם מַלְכֵּ֔נוּ הֶחֱל֥וּ שָׂרִ֖ים חֲמַ֣ת מִיָּ֑יִן מָשַׁ֥ךְ יָד֖וֹ אֶת־לֹצְצִֽים׃
verses.king_james: In the day of our king the princes have made him sick with bottles of wine; he stretched out his hand with scorners.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia applicuerunt quasi clibanum cor suum,  cum insidiaretur eis ;  tota nocte dormivit coquens eos :  mane ipse succensus quasi ignis flammæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־קֵרְב֧וּ כַתַּנּ֛וּר לִבָּ֖ם בְּאׇרְבָּ֑ם כׇּל־הַלַּ֙יְלָה֙ יָשֵׁ֣ן אֹֽפֵהֶ֔ם בֹּ֕קֶר ה֥וּא בֹעֵ֖ר כְּאֵ֥שׁ לֶהָבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: For they have made ready their heart like an oven, whiles they lie in wait: their baker sleepeth all the night; in the morning it burneth as a flaming fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes calefacti sunt quasi clibanus,  et devoraverunt judices suos :  omnes reges eorum ceciderunt ;  non est qui clamat in eis ad me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֻּלָּ֤ם יֵחַ֙מּוּ֙ כַּתַּנּ֔וּר וְאָכְל֖וּ אֶת־שֹׁפְטֵיהֶ֑ם כׇּל־מַלְכֵיהֶ֣ם נָפָ֔לוּ אֵין־קֹרֵ֥א בָהֶ֖ם אֵלָֽי׃
verses.king_james: They are all hot as an oven, and have devoured their judges; all their kings are fallen: there is none among them that calleth unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ephraim in populis ipse commiscebatur ;  Ephraim factus est subcinericius panis, qui non reversatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶפְרַ֕יִם בָּעַמִּ֖ים ה֣וּא יִתְבּוֹלָ֑ל אֶפְרַ֛יִם הָיָ֥ה עֻגָ֖ה בְּלִ֥י הֲפוּכָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Ephraim, he hath mixed himself among the people; Ephraim is a cake not turned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Comederunt alieni robur ejus, et ipse nescivit ;  sed et cani effusi sunt in eo, et ipse ignoravit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָכְל֤וּ זָרִים֙ כֹּח֔וֹ וְה֖וּא לֹ֣א יָדָ֑ע גַּם־שֵׂיבָה֙ זָ֣רְקָה בּ֔וֹ וְה֖וּא לֹ֥א יָדָֽע׃
verses.king_james: Strangers have devoured his strength, and he knoweth it not: yea, gray hairs are here and there upon him, yet he knoweth not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et humiliabitur superbia Israël in facie ejus ;  nec reversi sunt ad Dominum Deum suum,  et non quæsierunt eum in omnibus his.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעָנָ֥ה גְאֽוֹן־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל בְּפָנָ֑יו וְלֹא־שָׁ֙בוּ֙ אֶל־יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהֵיהֶ֔ם וְלֹ֥א בִקְשֻׁ֖הוּ בְּכׇל־זֹֽאת׃
verses.king_james: And the pride of Israel testifieth to his face: and they do not return to the Lord their God, nor seek him for all this.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factus est Ephraim quasi columba seducta non habens cor.  Ægyptum invocabant ; ad Assyrios abierunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י אֶפְרַ֔יִם כְּיוֹנָ֥ה פוֹתָ֖ה אֵ֣ין לֵ֑ב מִצְרַ֥יִם קָרָ֖אוּ אַשּׁ֥וּר הָלָֽכוּ׃
verses.king_james: Ephraim also is like a silly dove without heart: they call to Egypt, they go to Assyria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum profecti fuerint,  expandam super eos rete meum :  quasi volucrem cæli detraham eos ;  cædam eos secundum auditionem cœtus eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר יֵלֵ֗כוּ אֶפְר֤וֹשׂ עֲלֵיהֶם֙ רִשְׁתִּ֔י כְּע֥וֹף הַשָּׁמַ֖יִם אוֹרִידֵ֑ם אַיְסִירֵ֕ם כְּשֵׁ֖מַע לַעֲדָתָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: When they shall go, I will spread my net upon them; I will bring them down as the fowls of the heaven; I will chastise them, as their congregation hath heard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ eis, quoniam recesserunt a me !  vastabuntur, quia prævaricati sunt in me,  et ego redemi eos,  et ipsi locuti sunt contra me mendacia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: א֤וֹי לָהֶם֙ כִּֽי־נָדְד֣וּ מִמֶּ֔נִּי שֹׁ֥ד לָהֶ֖ם כִּי־פָ֣שְׁעוּ בִ֑י וְאָנֹכִ֣י אֶפְדֵּ֔ם וְהֵ֕מָּה דִּבְּר֥וּ עָלַ֖י כְּזָבִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Woe unto them! for they have fled from me: destruction unto them! because they have transgressed against me: though I have redeemed them, yet they have spoken lies against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non clamaverunt ad me in corde suo,  sed ululabant in cubilibus suis :  super triticum et vinum ruminabant ;  recesserunt a me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־זָעֲק֤וּ אֵלַי֙ בְּלִבָּ֔ם כִּ֥י יְיֵלִ֖ילוּ עַל־מִשְׁכְּבוֹתָ֑ם עַל־דָּגָ֧ן וְתִיר֛וֹשׁ יִתְגּוֹרָ֖רוּ יָס֥וּרוּ בִֽי׃
verses.king_james: And they have not cried unto me with their heart, when they howled upon their beds: they assemble themselves for corn and wine, and they rebel against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego erudivi eos, et confortavi brachia eorum,  et in me cogitaverunt malitiam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֣י יִסַּ֔רְתִּי חִזַּ֖קְתִּי זְרוֹעֹתָ֑ם וְאֵלַ֖י יְחַשְּׁבוּ־רָֽע׃
verses.king_james: Though I have bound and strengthened their arms, yet do they imagine mischief against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reversi sunt ut essent absque jugo ;  facti sunt quasi arcus dolosus :  cadent in gladio principes eorum, a furore linguæ suæ.  Ista subsannatio eorum in terra Ægypti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָשׁ֣וּבוּ ׀ לֹ֣א עָ֗ל הָיוּ֙ כְּקֶ֣שֶׁת רְמִיָּ֔ה יִפְּל֥וּ בַחֶ֛רֶב שָׂרֵיהֶ֖ם מִזַּ֣עַם לְשׁוֹנָ֑ם ז֥וֹ לַעְגָּ֖ם בְּאֶ֥רֶץ מִצְרָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: They return, but not to the most High: they are like a deceitful bow: their princes shall fall by the sword for the rage of their tongue: this shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.
